name: Weak Problem Definition
description: Buyer pain is not converted into a compelling problem with measurable consequence.
summary: Buyer has not established a clear, measurable problem with meaningful consequences
diagnostic_questions: What consequence is associated with the problem, How would the business measure this problem, Why would the business prioritize solving this problem?
root_cause_themes: A relevant business problem and desired outcome were not defined in terms meaningful to the buyer
Consequences of inaction were not articulated or owned by a buyer with influence

name: Outcome Misalignment
description: Buyer has unclear or misaligned desired outcomes, leading to feature-driven conversations.
summary: Buyer stakeholders are not aligned on desired outcomes or success criteria
diagnostic_questions: What does success look like, What future outcomes would justify making a change?
root_cause_themes: Seller value framing was not anchored to buyer success criteria
Sales conversations were focused on features rather than outcomes
Stakeholder outcomes or decision criteria were misaligned across the consensus

name: Commodity Relegation
description: Buyer decision criteria reduce all solutions to interchangeable options, minimizing differentiation and value.
summary: Decision criteria favor commodity capabilities or alternatives over your differentiated strengths
diagnostic_questions: How has the buyer defined solution selection criteria, Which buyer problems or outcomes are driving the evaluation, What aspects of the solution has the buyer described as meaningfully different or valuable?
root_cause_themes: Decision criteria were not shaped around differentiated outcomes
Internal advocacy did not influence how alternatives were evaluated

name: Champion Absence
description: No individual is coaching, influencing, or advocating internally.
summary: No internal advocate is actively driving the decision forward
diagnostic_questions: Who is selling internally on our behalf, Which buyer benefits most from the change, Do supportive buyers meet champion criteria of power, influence, and EB access?
root_cause_themes: No qualified internal buyer with power
influence
and EB access was identified
Champion commitment was assumed without behavioral evidence
Engagement or rapport was treated as proof of advocacy
No strategic action was requested to validate champion influence
Trust and alignment required for advocacy were not established
Champion advocacy was not enabled to shape internal alignment and decisions

name: Single‑Threaded Risk
description: Only one buyer contact is engaged over a period of time, limiting consensus creation.
summary: Only one stakeholder is engaged, limiting consensus formation
diagnostic_questions: Are we repeatedly meeting the same person without expanding stakeholder access, Are there decision-makers or influencers we have not engaged, Has our contact introduced us to additional stakeholders?
root_cause_themes: Engagement was limited to a single buyer
Stakeholder expansion was not intentionally pursued
Shared decision ownership was not established

name: Messaging Disconnect
description: Buyer pushes back on claims, value, or relevance early.
summary: Buyer reactions indicate the message is not relevant or compelling to their priorities
diagnostic_questions: Is the buyer pushing back on positioning or value claims, Is the buyer unclear on solution relevance to their priorities, Is the buyer misinterpreting what the solution enables or solves?
root_cause_themes: Solution value was not translated into persona-specific priorities
Messaging lacked personal relevance to the individual buyer

name: Competitive Mindshare
description: Competitor influence appears in buyer language, criteria, or behavior.
summary: Buyer language or evaluation steps are leaning toward a competitor’s approach
diagnostic_questions: Is the buyer signaling growing interest in a competitor, Are decision criteria being shaped around competitor strengths, Is the champion unable to influence internal narratives toward our solution?
root_cause_themes: Decision criteria were shaped by competitors
incumbents
or alternative champions before seller framing
Champion advocacy did not influence alternative perception
Competing solutions or priorities gained internal influence without coordinated champion response

name: Process Misalignment
description: Buyer in process conflict, or wants validation steps that do not support your strengths.
summary: Requested decision steps or validation approach do not align to your strengths
diagnostic_questions: Is the buyer driving a decision process that conflicts with how we win, Have evaluation and purchase steps been confirmed with someone in authority, Are we reacting to requests without a mutually agreed decision path?
root_cause_themes: Decision process steps were not fully identified or validated early
Changes in the decision process were not reconciled

name: EB Alignment Gap
description: Economic Buyer hasn't been engaged or has not validated their alignment and support of the purchase.
summary: Economic Buyer is not engaged or aligned to the initiative
diagnostic_questions: Who has discretionary authority to approve this spend, Has the economic buyer validated alignment on the initiative, Has access to the economic buyer been declined or repeatedly delayed?
root_cause_themes: Economic buyer was not aligned on the case for change
solution
investment
or timing
Economic buyer engagement was delayed
Economic buyer priorities and constraints were not understood
Progress was attempted without champion-mediated EB alignment
EB alignment gaps were not addressed through champion strategy

name: Stagnant Deal
description: We never built movement to begin with. Activity ≠ progress.
summary: Activity is occurring, but the deal is not progressing structurally
diagnostic_questions: Is the deal progressing without qualification progress, Are required stakeholders or decision steps not advancing, Is the buyer unclear on next steps?
root_cause_themes: A business-critical problem was not owned by the buyer
Urgency tied to cost of inaction was not established
Stakeholder expansion across personas and use cases did not occur

name: Momentum Loss
description: Movement existed but then declined or stopped.
summary: Engagement pace or responsiveness is declining across interactions
diagnostic_questions: Has buyer responsiveness slowed, Has urgency decreased compared to earlier conversations, Has tone or engagement become hesitant?
root_cause_themes: Internal prioritization weakened against competing initiatives
Champion-led advancement of key deal gaps was not tested
Momentum remained seller-driven
Decision ownership and next-step accountability were not reinforced
Case for change was not presented to sufficient influencers

name: Validation Disadvantage
description: Buyer structures validation event around competitor strengths, incumbent approaches, or commodity requirements that erode the seller’s differentiation.
summary: Validation approach favors competitor strengths or avoids your differentiation
diagnostic_questions: Is the validation plan misaligned with our differentiated strengths, Are test criteria favoring competitor or incumbent capabilities, Are we being evaluated on commodity requirements?
root_cause_themes: Validation criteria were defined around competitor or incumbent capabilities
No champion-led alignment shaped evaluation around differentiated outcomes
Sales engagement began after validation structure was already defined
Seller followed buyer validation path without reframing around differentiated outcomes

name: Late Stakeholder Risk
description: A new persona appears late and disrupts scope, criteria, or priorities.
summary: New stakeholders are entering late and introducing new requirements or objections
diagnostic_questions: Has a late stakeholder introduced new friction, Does this stakeholder have influence or veto power, Is the champion unable to align or neutralize this stakeholder?
root_cause_themes: Decision process stakeholders were not identified or engaged early
Stakeholder decision criteria were not validated through a champion
Late stakeholder introduced new priorities or risks

name: Commercial Misalignment
description: Pricing or terms conflict emerges due to missing metrics, weak value, or lack of EB alignment.
summary: Commercial discussions are misaligned on value, pricing, or terms
diagnostic_questions: Is there friction between pricing or terms and buyer expectations, Is value perception misaligned with the proposal, Has procurement escalated issues unrelated to impact or value?
root_cause_themes: Commercial expectations were not aligned to buyer value and success criteria
Investment justification was not champion-sponsored before commercial discussion
Conflicting stakeholders introduced commercial risk
Seller and champion did not align on negotiation interests and boundaries
Value thresholds and anchor points were not aligned
Seller reacted to positional demands instead of interest-based options

name: Adoption Without Conversion
description: Product usage is present but has not translated into ownership, decision process, or commercial intent.
summary: Usage exists, but no structured buying motion or ownership is forming.
diagnostic_questions: Is anyone responsible for driving this internally, Has usage triggered a formal evaluation or buying process, Are stakeholders or commercial steps forming around this usage?
root_cause_themes: Usage is not tied to a compelling business problem
No internal owner has taken responsibility
Usage has not created urgency or visibility

name: Unqualified Deal
description: No structural foundation exists to justify continued pursuit of the opportunity.
summary: A deal is active but lacks the core structural conditions required for a viable decision path.
diagnostic_questions: Has a buyer of authority confirmed a compelling problem worth solving?
Is there a stakeholder willing to sponsor and drive the initiative internally?
Is there a credible path to Economic Buyer alignment or support?
root_cause_themes: No sponsor-backed case for change
No Champion ownership
No Economic Buyer alignment or access

name: High Champion Advocacy
description: Champion is actively coaching, influencing, and guiding the internal process.
summary: Champion is actively advocating, coaching, and driving the deal internally
diagnostic_questions: Is a buyer proactively selling internally on our behalf, Is the champion creating access and insight beyond seller requests, Is the champion taking unprompted actions demonstrating deal ownership?
root_cause_themes: Champion owns an outcome-driven case for change aligned to personal priorities
A trusted champion with credibility and influence is positioned to sponsor the solution internally

name: EB Sponsorship Motion
description: EB leans in, validating direction and impact.
summary: Economic Buyer is engaged and sponsoring the initiative
diagnostic_questions: Is the economic buyer directly engaged and supportive, Has the economic buyer validated the problem, outcomes, and investment, Is the economic buyer creating urgency or alignment across stakeholders?
root_cause_themes: Economic buyer has validated the problem
outcomes
and investment as business priorities
Champion has influenced the economic buyer to engage as sponsor
Economic buyer supports the purchase but lacks consensus-backed execution confidence

name: Consensus Expansion Momentum
description: New stakeholders are proactively added to expand and strengthen support.
summary: Stakeholder engagement is expanding and consensus is forming
diagnostic_questions: Are stakeholders being added proactively with positive context, Is access expanding across functions tied to our differentiated outcomes, Are buyers accelerating multi-threading toward selection?
root_cause_themes: Stakeholder engagement is driven by an internal sponsor
Additional stakeholders are engaging as value is recognized
Consensus expansion is limited by unaddressed stakeholder trust criteria

name: High Responsiveness Momentum
description: Communication accelerates, indicating internal urgency and strong fit.
summary: Buyer engagement is accelerating with faster responses and next steps
diagnostic_questions: Is the buyer consistently responsive in communication, Are tasks or data returned with urgency, Is responsiveness increasing as the decision approaches?
root_cause_themes: Champion-sponsored case for change is prioritized as urgent
Decision timing is aligned across stakeholders
Stakeholder communication is accelerating internal alignment

name: Validation Advantage
description: Buyer structures validation around your solution’s strengths.
summary: Validation steps are aligned to your differentiated capabilities
diagnostic_questions: Is the validation plan aligned to our strengths, Are evaluation criteria being shaped in our favor, Are competitors struggling to meet the criteria?
root_cause_themes: Validation is co-designed with a champion aligned to success criteria
Evaluation criteria favor differentiated outcomes
Stakeholders collaborate on validation aligned to solution strengths
Validation confirms preferred solution but commercial alignment is incomplete

name: Adoption Converting to Decision
description: Product usage is delivering meaningful value and converting into ownership, stakeholder alignment, and a structured buying process
summary: Usage is delivering value and expanding into a defined decision path with internal ownership and commercial awareness
diagnostic_questions: Is the product delivering meaningful value to the business, Is there a clear internal owner driving this forward, Has usage converted into a defined buying process?
root_cause_themes: Product value is experienced directly through usage
An internal owner has taken responsibility for advancing the initiative
Stakeholders are aligning around outcomes and commercial progression
